data.title: Tokenize People as a non-house section color

data.body: People is a deferred (hidden-from-launch) non-house section with a regular {light:#C49040, deep:#B65D19} pair, but its hex is hardcoded in 3 places: PeoplePage (bg/accent), Navbar nav-items (#C49040), OctahedronHero faces (#C49040). Same architecture as houses: OctahedronHero feeds three.js (needs REAL hex), so People needs a JS data home as real hex + a derived @theme token, synced. Introduce a non-house 'section' concept (single real-hex source — extend houses.ts or a small sections file), add --color-section-people-{light,deep} @theme tokens mirroring it, repoint all 3 sites, extend the sync test to cover sections, regen conformance baseline, document People as a deferred non-house section. Story is a separate follow-up (LIVE + irregular 3rd color #D4BA58 that needs a design decision).

Cold review (independent sub-agent, fresh context) vs plan acceptance: PASS, no findings. People hex (C49040/B65D19) now in ONE source (houses.ts SECTIONS) + mirrored theme tokens; zero in PeoplePage/Navbar/OctahedronHero/tests. Token values equal old hex (no visual change). JS-vs-CSS correct: Navbar + OctahedronHero read SECTIONS.people.light (real hex; no var leak); PeoplePage uses bg-section-people-light + var for inline. section-tokens-sync is non-vacuous (mutation-tested: drift fails, orphan fails, restored); checks light+deep, hex-equality, missing+orphan, pair count. Gates: typecheck/build/conformance pass; tests 7 failed/159 passed (the 7 = FRAC-199 set, +4 = new test, no new failures); house-tokens-sync still 14/14. DESIGN.md count 16 to 18 (4 surface + 12 house + 2 section). HOUSES untouched. Move to done.
